codeReview.summary: All hardcoded hex colors and raw Tailwind color classes across the three targeted admin pages (FinanceSupportPage, SupportTicketsPage, PortalInsightsPage) have been replaced with CSS variable tokens. Four new tokens (--color-accent, --color-accent-icon, --color-link, --color-link-hover) were added to variables.css with proper dark mode variants. A comprehensive grep sweep of ALL 13 admin page files confirms zero remaining hardcoded hex values or raw Tailwind color utility classes (text-*, bg-*, border-*, ring-*). No regressions introduced.

issues.description: The link element has 'className="text-[13px] hover:underline flex items-center gap-1.5"' with a static 'style={{ color: "var(--color-link)" }}'. The hover:underline is preserved, but there is no hover color transition to --color-link-hover. The --color-link-hover token exists in variables.css but is unused. This is a minor polish gap — not a functional issue since the underline provides sufficient hover feedback.
Same pattern as above — mobile 'View details' button uses style={{ color: 'var(--color-link)' }} with hover:underline but no hover color change to --color-link-hover.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CSS variable token system has grown to include --color-accent, --color-accent-icon, --color-link, --color-link-hover, --color-success-icon, --color-warning-icon, --color-error-icon. These semantic tokens are the approved way to apply colors in admin pages. The AGENTS.md convention 'CSS Variables ONLY — use var(--color-*) for all colors' is correct and sufficient.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 13 admin pages verified clean of hardcoded colors via grep sweep. variables.css defines 50+ color tokens across light and dark modes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Inline style={{ color: 'var(--color-*)' }} is the established pattern for applying CSS variable colors to individual elements where Tailwind arbitrary value syntax (text-[var(--color-*)]) might be verbose. Both patterns coexist in the codebase. No convention gap — just noting the pattern for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FinanceSupportPage uses inline style={{ color: 'var(--color-success-icon)' }} on icon elements, while other elements use className='text-[var(--color-text-primary)]' Tailwind arbitrary value syntax.

summary: PASS. The feature fully delivers on its promise: all hardcoded hex colors (#22c55e, #f59e0b, #ef4444, #8b5cf6) and raw Tailwind color classes (text-emerald-600, text-blue-600, text-violet-600, text-amber-600, text-red-500) have been replaced with CSS variable tokens across FinanceSupportPage, SupportTicketsPage, and PortalInsightsPage. Four new CSS tokens were added to variables.css with proper dark mode values. A comprehensive grep sweep of all 13 admin page files confirms zero remaining hardcoded colors. The only minor observation is that the --color-link-hover token was added but not wired into hover states on links — the hover:underline provides visual feedback but no color transition. TypeScript compilation passes clean. No regressions detected.
